FR-001.title: Adopt Codex hybrid theme: owned light/dark palette, host fonts/accessibility inherited

FR-002.title: All text roles meet WCAG AA in both palettes

FR-003.title: Code blocks readable on owned surface in both themes

FR-004.title: Primitives migrate to Codex roles without API/message changes

FR-005.title: Overview is default landing view; document fallback when no activity

FR-006.title: Document rail (where) separated from completion marks (how far)

FR-007.title: Rail/steps fully workflow-driven, no stock-four assumption

FR-008.title: CTA uses workflow-derived label; extra commands in Other actions

FR-009.title: Every lifecycle state renders correct badge/marks/actions

FR-010.title: All markdown content types styled via existing emitted classes

FR-011.title: Hide empty meta/template remnants; branch metadata contrast

FR-012.title: Three-column grid with documented responsive behavior

FR-013.title: Inline review comments + refinement queue keep working

FR-014.title: Living-specs mode behavior preserved in new shell

FR-016.title: Recovery/safety affordances surface in new shell

FR-017.title: Stories and viewer docs updated with the design

context: constraint: docs/viewer-states.md state machine must hold unchanged
constraint: markdown emitted-class contract and extension<->webview message protocol are frozen interfaces
constraint: keep inline-comment UX (line-anchored comments + refinement queue)
intent: Ship the Codex redesign (owned palette, Overview-first shell, workflow-driven rail, data-display skin) in the production viewer with zero behavioral regressions
expectations: sidebar tree, spec-editor, workflow-editor webviews unchanged
approach: Reskin-and-relayout over frozen seams: re-value the existing token layer to the Codex owned light/dark palette, recompose App.tsx into the shell regions (header + view switch, grouped document rail, 72ch reading column, run-facts aside, CTA footer with Other-actions menu) with each region kept on its existing component and state seam, and restyle the markdown pipeline's emitted classes for the data-display skin. Signals, message protocol, state derivation, and viewer-states transitions untouched.

decisions.decision: adopt the palette by re-valuing the existing token layer
decisions.why: every partial already consumes these token names; one coherent restyle, no rename churn
decisions.rejected: shipping codex.css alongside and porting components onto .cx-* classes

decisions.decision: shell regions map 1:1 onto existing components (SpecHeader/NavigationBar/FooterActions + new RunAside)
decisions.why: components already own the right state seams; markup/CSS change only, protocol and state machine fenced
decisions.rejected: a fresh shell component tree replacing them

decisions.decision: Overview replaces the Activity toggle, defaulting by data presence; living mode exempt
decisions.why: panel is already a peer region behind a signal — default-state change delivers the design cheaply
decisions.rejected: keeping the toggle and only restyling it

decisions.decision: code keeps highlight.js on an owned always-dark surface in both themes
decisions.why: the accepted proposal fixes light-mode code without touching the highlighting pipeline
decisions.rejected: per-theme syntax palettes overriding hljs classes

decisions.decision: host typography (Geist retired) and 6px geometry supersede the 2px terminal direction; docs updated
decisions.why: the selected design inherits editor fonts and softer geometry; docs must not describe the old look
decisions.rejected: keeping Geist lead / keeping 2px radii under the new palette

plan.summary: Full plan + research + data-model + UI contract for adopting the Codex design over frozen viewer seams
plan.key_finding: Every Codex region maps onto an existing component and state seam (getApproveLabel, enhancementButtons, hasAnyData, livingMode), so the entire redesign is markup+CSS with zero protocol or derivation changes

tasks.summary: 30 tasks, 8 phases: tokens foundation -> US1 data-display skin (8-way parallel wave) -> US2 lifecycle verification -> US3 Overview-first shell (5-way component wave) -> US4 custom workflows -> US5 layout+comments -> polish/docs/reconciliation
